Container.Name: alejandrothegreat
Container.Overview:
Discord music bot with a web dashboard, called Alejandrothegreat. Paste a Spotify link/playlist or just search terms and it plays the matching audio from YouTube (Spotify doesn't allow third-party bots to stream its own audio). Control it with Discord slash commands, `!`-prefix text commands, or by logging into the web dashboard with your Discord account.
Everything - the Discord bot token, Spotify credentials, and an admin password - is configured through a setup wizard at http://[IP]:[PORT:3005]/setup.html after you start the container. No environment variables required.
You'll need a free Discord application (discord.com/developers/applications) and, optionally, a free Spotify application (developer.spotify.com/dashboard) before running the wizard - see the project README for the exact steps.
Container.ExtraSearchTerms: spotify youtube music player queue playlist discord bot dashboard
Container.Requires: Requires a free Discord application (discord.com/developers/applications) with a bot token, Client ID, and Client Secret. A free Spotify application (developer.spotify.com/dashboard) is optional but improves Spotify link/playlist search. Both are entered through the in-app setup wizard after install - see the README for exact steps.
Container.Changes: ### 2026-07 - Default port changed from 3000 to 3005. - Appdata path simplified to /mnt/user/appdata/alejandrothegreat (no nested /data subfolder). - Initial Community Applications template.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Port for the web dashboard and setup wizard

Config.Name: Data
Config.Description: Stores the SQLite database with your bot credentials, admin password, and per-server settings. Back this up.
